channel.title: Comments for page "Permanent Waves"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Permanent Waves"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was honestly a really good read. Hope to see a continuation of this story!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Collabflict" is now one of my new favorite words, and also perfectly encapsulates the entire tone of this series (or at least, this part of it1).

Footnotes
1. Yes, there will be other tales with Thorne and Spencer following the memetic bomber arc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do like Agent Green, and he is definitely best used in collabflict with other professionals. Enjoyable friction, and I look forward to the plot ramping up in the next instalment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ngl, half the fun of writing this was having Green and Spencer clash egos, and it's kind of the driving force of this tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know what I like better. Green and Spencer clashing egos or the idea that Three Portlands residents have anti-Foundation signs just sitting around in the event they need to protest. Either way its a fun tale that really does a good job showing UIU/Foundation collaboration. Looking forward to seeing what happens next.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The UIU Series With No Name was one of my major influences in how the UIU is depicted in Third Law (the other major influence being TwistedGear's UIU series), so it means a lot to know you enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You, sir, are stealing my THUNDER. And also reminding me that I lost track on my UIU series thing because of hiatuses and bad ideas and blugh.

I enjoy this, though. I look forward to more. And I seriously need to get back to writing The UIU series with No Name

item.title: Album Title Goes Here
item.content:encoded:
Hey, remember when I said I was going to write a UIU police procedural set in Three Portlands? Yeah, this is it. Expect A Farewell to Kings to come out sometime between now and later.

Thanks to Randomini, Conwell, Apoplexic, Aiden Eldritch, TyGently, and Adnol for critique and commentary.
